prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for furan using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (PySCF RKS; exact four-centre integrals, no density fitting), tier 'standard', gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 cation state was computed at charge +1, multiplicity 2 (doublet); the N+1 anion state at charge −1, multiplicity 2 (doublet). Fukui indices were derived from finite differences of Mulliken partial charges (PySCF). The integrity check reports status='ok', trustworthy=true; sum(f+)=1.0000 and sum(f-)=1.0000, confirming charge conservation. No warnings were reported. The headline value reported is the largest f+ (electrophilic Fukui index), located on atom 5 (C, beta-carbon): f+ = 1.9242931. The most nucleophilic site by f- is also atom 5: f- = 0.1616371. The dual descriptor at atom 5 is 1.762656 (positive, electrophile-preferred). Oxygen (atom 1) has a negative dual descriptor (−1.415440), consistent with its nucleophilic character. Note that Mulliken-partitioned Fukui indices can yield values outside [0,1] — the relative ordering carries the physical meaning. Output artifacts: result JSON 'furan_fukui_dft.json'; bar chart at the run directory. CLI: cli.py fukui furan.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1.
